assertions.expectation: Clearly states that GKE auto-upgrades clusters automatically as the default and recommended model — this is what differentiates GKE from self-managed Kubernetes
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'GKE's auto-upgrade model is the recommended approach' and explains that auto-upgrades are GKE's core value proposition for automated lifecycle management

assertions.expectation: Recommends configuring maintenance windows to control WHEN auto-upgrades happen (e.g., weekends, off-peak hours)
assertions.reason: Provides specific gcloud commands for setting maintenance windows with off-peak hours and weekly Sunday scheduling

assertions.expectation: Recommends maintenance exclusions to control WHAT auto-upgrades happen (e.g., 'no minor or node upgrades' to block disruptive changes while allowing security patches)
assertions.reason: Provides detailed gcloud command for adding 'no_minor_or_node_upgrades' exclusion scope and explains how this allows security patches while blocking disruptive changes

assertions.expectation: Does NOT recommend disabling auto-upgrades or switching to a purely manual upgrade cadence as the first option
assertions.reason: Response explicitly argues against manual scheduling, stating it 'creates more work, potential for human error, and defeats the purpose of managed Kubernetes'

assertions.expectation: Explains that manual upgrades are useful for specific scenarios (emergency patches, accelerating the schedule) but are the exception, not the rule
assertions.reason: Includes a specific section 'When to Use Manual Upgrades' listing emergency security patching, accelerating for specific features, and upgrading deliberately held-back clusters as exceptions

assertions.expectation: Recommends enrolling in scheduled upgrade notifications (72h advance) for visibility into upcoming auto-upgrades
assertions.reason: Response does not mention upgrade notifications or 72-hour advance notice for visibility into upcoming auto-upgrades

assertions.expectation: Suggests checking the GKE release schedule for longer-range planning (~1 month best-case for new minors)
assertions.reason: Response does not mention checking GKE release schedules or provide timeline information for longer-range planning of minor version releases

assertions.expectation: Mentions release channel selection as the primary lever for upgrade cadence (Stable = slower, Regular = balanced)
assertions.reason: Response acknowledges the user is already on Regular channel and calls it 'perfect for production', implying understanding of channel-based cadence control, though it doesn't explicitly compare all channel options
